Sport|Olympics

Therowingworld descends on the Vaires-sur-Marne Nautical Stadium to compete for Olympic glory over eight days of action atParis 2024.

There are set to be 502 athletes competing across 14 different events, with the schedule brought to a close by the men’s and women’s eight finals on August 3.

Team GB have enjoyed plenty of rowing success in the past but the Tokyo Games failed to deliver in the same way, as the British rowers registered their worst Olympic performance in 49 years.

Only two medals were won, silver in the men’s quadruple sculls and men’s eight bronze. However hopes are high heading into theParis Olympics-  the men’s eight crew are the reigning world champions and the women’s four, includingHelen Glover, arrive in France in superb form.

Here is the full 2024 Olympics rowing schedule (all times BST)...
